message:
perf(vdbe,func): reduce allocations and hot-path overhead in opcode loop

The VDBE engine's inner loop was spending time on unnecessary work:
- Every register write went through bounds-checking and resize logic even
  though the register file is pre-allocated; add set_reg_fast() for the
  common case (Integer/Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Rem opcodes).
- MakeRecord and Function opcodes cloned registers into fresh Vecs; now
  they borrow directly from the register slice.
- Move opcode cloned-then-nulled source registers; use mem::replace.
- once_flags used a Vec<bool> (1 byte/op); replace with a bitset that
  lives on the stack for programs <= 256 opcodes.
- Distinct-aggregate key buffers always heap-allocated; use SmallVec<64>
  to keep small keys on the stack (new smallvec dependency).
- trace_opcode() was called unconditionally; gate behind trace_opcodes
  flag check.
- Function opcode did per-call Instant::now() timing + tracing even when
  nobody was listening; split into record_func_call_count_only() fast
  path and gate tracing behind trace_opcodes.
- Function registry lookup re-uppercased names every call; add
  find_scalar_precanonical() / find_aggregate_precanonical() to skip
  canonicalization when codegen already emits uppercase names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
